author: barnabasbusa
content: can clients please let me know what flag to use to enable multiplexed builders?

author: bharath.vedartham
content: this is for builder-api right? outside prysm not sure if any other client has implemented it

author: barnabasbusa
content: whats the flags for prysm?

author: bharath.vedartham
barnabasbusa
content: this feels very non ux friendly. Can we have vc flags for these pls?

author: nflaig
content: what do you mean, this should be on the bn?

author: barnabasbusa
content: I thought all config will be running in the vc going forward?

author: nflaig
content: so the vc connects to builders? that's new

author: bharath.vedartham
content:
to build the request auth and sign it, you ll need the VC to know the urls right?
the VC calls to CL to get a block with the request auth to send to the builder, the CL queries the bids from the builder and sends the beacon block with the best bid to the VC. VC signs it and sends to CL for broadcast

author: bharath.vedartham
content: i guess the flags are anyways client specific, its up to clients on how they want to implement it

author: bharath.vedartham
content: I propose that we should target all clients having builder-api impl by devnet-7 so that we can test it in all clients before going to a testnet.

author: nflaig
content: yeah that could work, I am generally in favor if we can move this config to the vc side, this also means you can have different builders per validator/pubkey which could be useful for some, it's a lot different to what we have today though since the urls the beacon node has to query can vary between each proposal depending on what the vc has configured. so the beacon node learns about what urls to query when the vc submit the `BuilderPreferences` beforehand?

author: bharath.vedartham
content: yeah you could do that, you could store the list of urls in the beacon node from there. But I think a better idea probably would be to presign the request auth's in the VC based on your duties and send them along with the request to get the beacon block from the CL

author: bharath.vedartham
content: the auths anywyas contains the url, so you can just query those urls

author: nflaig
content:
> send them along with the request to get the beacon block from the CL
so you want to send that as part of `produceBlockV4` request?

author: bharath.vedartham
content: that makes sense to me, like this is how prysm is doing it : https://github.com/OffchainLabs/prysm/pull/16961/changes#diff-20de6d51bd5bdb24dcd8f3634f1a06f933e6b5049c9502bb0c634b5ec6304724R85

author: bharath.vedartham
content: requset auth are specific for a given slot, so you could just presign them and send it along with the `produceBlock` request

author: nflaig
content: why is it not fine to submit them via `BuilderPreferences`? we anyways need to do that beforehand, didn't builders say they don't want these details JIT which is why we introduced the builder preferences in the first place?

author: bharath.vedartham
content:
okay i think we are not on the same page. The `BuilderPreferences` def need to be sent apriori like you mentioned.
We also need to submit a request auth when requesting a bid from the builder which happens when the slot starts. so when you are the proposer, you need to send the request auth for the slot to the builder

author: bharath.vedartham
content: and the request auth includes the slot, so you need to set it to the current slot

author: nflaig
content: but the signed auth you pass as part of the builder preferences aren't set to the current slot, isn't it the proposal slot?

author: nflaig
content: I need to think more about this but <@1386598056320831509> now you are leaking `SignedRequestAuthV1` into the beacon-api spec, I really don't like this, also how is this passed, as a hex encoded query string? (this is probably the wrong channel to discuss this, we can move to <#710831784991916072> channel)

author: nflaig
content: although we probably need this anyways since we need `BuilderPreferences` to be signed by the vc, then pass it to the beacon to forward it to the builder(s)

author: bharath.vedartham
content: ahh no i specified to be the slot at which we are sending it

author: bharath.vedartham
content: its to authenticate the builder preferences request being sent

author: bharath.vedartham
content: yes we can move it to the apis channel. this is passed in the getExecutionPayloadBid api in the request body either json encoded or ssz encoded

author: nflaig
content: shouldn't it be the proposal slot though?

author: nflaig
content: no I mean if you pass it as part of `produceBlockV4`, how the api call from beacon --> builder(s) looks like it clear to me

author: bharath.vedartham
content: not necessarily, i mean the point of the request auth is to authenticate the request for the given slot, so it is used to auth the builder preference req for that slot

author: bharath.vedartham
content: didn't get the question here? 😅

author: bharath.vedartham
content: we should probably take it to <#710831784991916072> as you suggested

author: nflaig
content: don't you wanna pass the signed auth request when you call produce block v4 on the beacon from the vc?

author: nflaig
content: your own PR says differently

author: nflaig
content: <@412614104222531604> can we roll out `deathstar`, I did some enhancements?

author: barnabasbusa
content: yeah how many nodes?

author: nflaig
content: `lodestar-ethrex-1` should be sufficient

author: bharath.vedartham
content: https://github.com/ethereum/builder-specs/blob/main/specs/gloas/validator.md?plain=1#L141 its the slot in which the request is sent basically

author: bharath.vedartham
content: i should updates the types description

author: bharath.vedartham
content: eitherways i m not sure about using the `proposal_slot` in the request auth when sending builder preferences

author: nflaig
content: why? this seems better to me, the validator basically says "these are my builder preferences for this proposal slot"

author: barnabasbusa
content: can roll it out in a few mins

author: bharath.vedartham
content:
i guess the request auth is always for signing the given request for the given slot. so semantically to me, it makes sense to set it to the slot for which you are sending the request. 
The whole purpose of the request auth in the builder preferences is for the builder to know that it is coming from the expected validator.

author: barnabasbusa
content: done

author: barnabasbusa
content: btw I noticed that other lodestar nodes are hardcoded to 078e689 commit. Do you want the latest commit on all lodestar ?

author: nflaig
content: ah yes, should all be on latest

author: barnabasbusa
content: rolling

author: nflaig
content:
> The whole purpose of the request auth in the builder preferences is for the builder to know that it is coming from the expected validator.
that has nothing to do with using current vs. proposal slot?

author: bharath.vedartham
content: yeah true, i initially spec'ed it to use the current slot(sans that types definition). I need to think about whether to use current or proposal slot

author: bharath.vedartham
content: i dont think there is fundamentally anything bad in using either of them

author: bharath.vedartham
content: maybe it just boils to semantics

author: nflaig
content: if we use current slot then need to specify how builders handle this, eg. if you submit it later, should builder reject? could be due to a clock skew or latency as well, but either works, my impression from the PR was that we use proposal slot though

author: bharath.vedartham
content:
yeah it is specified as the slot for which the request is being made: https://github.com/ethereum/builder-specs/blob/main/specs/gloas/validator.md?plain=1#L141
its more a generic request auth being sent, let me get back after some thought

author: bharath.vedartham
content: also you send the request usually at the start of the current slot, so i dont know if such cases like whether builder should reject matter? As long as the builder has your latest preference you are good. It really doesn't matter when you submit it

author: terencechain
content: i think we should have multiplexing but i have not personally test it

author: terencechain
content: yes, the plan is to have everything on the VC side because of auth

content: I have an open pr wiht multiple buildoor support on ethereum package

content: can you let me know how I can test it with prysm? ANd i'll merge it in once confirmed to be working
